spec_match.rationale: The concept page successfully maps out the security trust hierarchy across different origins for SVG, CSS, and backdrop filters.

interactive.rationale: The user can toggle between filter types and hover over rows to dynamically view specific rungs of the trust ladder.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns cleanly with the feature description of blocked SVG filters on plugin and iframe boundary types.

edge_cases.rationale: It thoroughly covers sandbox status, srcdoc, plugin embeds, and data URIs which are all critical edge cases of this feature.

references.rationale: The page does not cite the required chromestatus.com/feature/5117170452398080 reference link directly, though it links to sibling demos.

design_system.rationale: The details are only revealed via mouse hover on .rung divs, which makes them inaccessible to keyboard and screen-reader users.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5117170452398080 in the references list or crumbs block.

openQuestions.title: Improve keyboard accessibility for ladder details
openQuestions.detail: The detailed information about each trust ladder rung is displayed using CSS hover on non-focusable <div> elements. Make the rows focusable (e.g., using tabindex="0") or turn them into `<button>` details/summary disclosure widgets so keyboard users can toggle and read the reasons.

summary: The Origin Trust Ladder is a brilliant conceptual breakdown of the security model changes in Chrome 150. It lacks the mandatory ChromeStatus link and has an accessibility limitation regarding hover-only details.
